title: High-throughput electroporation assembly
abstract: An electroporation assembly for interfacing with a liquid handling system includes at least two electrically conductive layer components and a channel layer component. The channel layer component is configured to be removably disposed between the electrically conductive layer components. The electrically conductive layer components and the channel layer component define a plurality of parallel electroporation flow paths in an assembled state. An electroporation system includes an electroporation assembly and a cell collection unit. The electroporation assembly is configured to direct a flow of a cell sample to the cell collection unit.
claims:
Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. An electroporation assembly for interfacing with a liquid handling system, comprising: at least two electrically conductive layer components; and a channel layer component configured to be removably disposed between the at least two electrically conductive layer components, the at least two electrically conductive layer components and the channel layer component defining a plurality of parallel electroporation flow paths in an assembled state. 2. The electroporation assembly of claim 1, further comprising at least one support layer component configured to interface with one of the at least two electrically conductive layer components. 3. The electroporation assembly of claim 2, wherein the at least one support layer component comprises an electrically insulating material. 4. The electroporation assembly of claim 2 or claim 3, wherein the at least one support layer component is configured to seal against the one of the at least two conductive layer components. 5. The electroporation assembly of any one of claims 2-4, further comprising a sealing layer component configured to interface with the at least one support layer component and the one of the at least two electrically conductive layer components. 6. The electroporation assembly of any one of claims 2-5, wherein the at least one support layer component is an inlet support layer component defining a plurality of input ports configured to receive cell sample fluid from a manual pipette, an automated liquid dispenser, or an automated liquid handling system. 7. The electroporation device of any one of claims 2-5, wherein the at least one support layer component is an inlet support layer component defining a plurality of input ports configured to connect with one or more cell sample reservoirs such that cell sample fluid entering the assembly from the one or more cell sample reservoirs is sealed. 

The electroporation assembly of any one of claims 2-5, wherein the at least one support layer component is an outlet support layer component defining a plurality of outlet ports. The electroporation assembly of claim 8, wherein the outlet support layer component is configured to connect with one or more collection reservoirs such that cell sample fluid exiting the assembly to the one or more collection reservoirs is sealed. The electroporation assembly of claim 8, wherein each of the plurality of outlet ports comprises a nozzle. The electroporation assembly of any one of claims 1-10, wherein one of the at least two conductive layer components comprises nozzles disposed at terminating ends of the electroporation flow paths, and wherein the plurality of output ports is configured to receive the nozzles. The electroporation assembly of any one of claims 1-11, wherein the at least two conductive layer components are removeable, reusable, interchangeable, autoclavable, or a combination thereof. The electroporation assembly of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the at least two conductive layer components are configured to produce an electric field within each of the plurality of electroporation flow paths. The electroporation assembly of any one of claims 1-12, wherein the at least two conductive layer components are configured to produce an electric field within a subset of the plurality of electroporation flow paths. The electroporation assembly of any one of claims 1-14, wherein at least one of the at least two conductive layer components is uniformly conductive. The electroporation assembly of any one of claims 1-14, wherein at least one of the at least two electrically conductive layer components comprises selectively activatable electrodes capable of producing electric fields that vary in at least one of strength and timing for a subset of the plurality of electroporation flow paths. The electroporation assembly of any one of claims 1-16, wherein one of the at least two conductive layer components comprises nozzles disposed at a terminating end of each of 

the plurality of parallel electroporation flow paths, the nozzles configured to direct a flow of a cell sample fluid travelling through each of the electroporation flow paths into corresponding receptacles of a cell collection unit. The electroporation assembly of any one of claims 1-17, wherein the channel layer component comprises an electrically insulating material. The electroporation assembly of any one of claims 1-18, wherein the channel layer component is removeable, reusable, interchangeable, autoclavable, or a combination thereof. The electroporation assembly of any one of claims 1-19, wherein the channel layer component defines a constriction region of each of the plurality of electroporation flow paths. The electroporation assembly of claim 20, wherein a geometry of the constriction region comprises a uniform cross-sectional area. The electroporation assembly of claim 20, wherein a geometry of the constriction region comprises a varying cross-sectional area. The electroporation assembly of claim 22, wherein the varying cross sectional area provides for a converging channel, a diverging channel, or a converging and diverging channel. The electroporation assembly of any one of claims 20-23, wherein the constriction regions defined by the channel layer geometrically vary for at least a subset of the plurality of electroporation flow paths. The electroporation assembly of any one of claims 1-24, wherein the channel layer component comprises at least two channel layer components. The electroporation assembly of claim 25, wherein each of the at least two channel layer components defines a portion of the plurality of electroporation flow paths. The electroporation assembly of claim 26, wherein the at least two channel layers provide for configurable constriction regions for at least a subset of the plurality of electroporation flow paths. - 18 - 

The electroporation assembly of any one of claims 1-27, wherein at least one of the conductive layer components comprises a sealing structure configured to engage with a complimentary sealing structure of the channel layer component. The electroporation assembly of claim 28, wherein the sealing structures are configured to be self-sealing in the assembled state. An electroporation system, comprising: a cell collection unit; and the electroporation assembly of any one of claims 1-29, wherein the electroporation assembly is configured to direct a flow of a cell sample to the cell collection unit. The electroporation system of claim 30, wherein the cell collection unit is a reservoir, a multi-reservoir device, or a multi-well plate. The electroporation system of claim 30 or claim 31, further comprising a liquid handling system, wherein the electroporation assembly is configured to receive a flow of a cell sample from the liquid handling system. The electroporation system of any one of claims 30-32, further comprising a power source in operative arrangement with the at least two electrically conductive layer components and configured to deliver a voltage differential thereto. The electroporation system of claim 33, wherein the power source and the at least two electrically conductive layers are configured to deliver electrical pulses to the plurality of electroporation flow paths. - 19 -
